author: mark
text:
Default bl flow is single-agent; review handoff is opt-in

Reframe SKILL.md so the canonical flow is one agent taking a task
claim → work → review → close → done, with review as a transient
checkpoint rather than a stopping point. Demote the split
submitter/reviewer pattern to an opt-in section that explicitly warns
against reaching for it unless a reviewer has actually been configured.
Add a matching stance paragraph near the top of README and tighten
the review/close glossary entries. Update the bl review output so it
points at the next step (bl close) instead of printing "submitted for
review", which nudged agents to stop short.
